stories.headline: Drone Hits Ship Near Qatar as US Awaits Iran Peace Plan Response
stories.summary: A drone strike briefly set a cargo vessel ablaze in the Persian Gulf, the latest shipping attack to test a fragile ceasefire as the US awaits Iran’s response to its proposal to end 10 weeks of war.

stories.headline: Why is Iran taking time to respond to the US proposal to end the war?
stories.summary: Iran continues to review the proposal as Tehran's nuclear programme and Strait of Hormuz remain main sticking points.

stories.headline: Iran responds to US proposal to end the war via a Pakistani mediator
stories.summary: If Iran agrees to it, the proposal from Washington will formally end the war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz before talks begin on more contentious issues, which include the Iranian nuclear programme.

stories.headline: Iran sends response to US proposals to end war
stories.summary: No details have been released of Iran's response - or the US proposals - designed to bring the war to an end.

stories.headline: Iran Responds to Latest U.S. Plan to End War
stories.summary: The two countries have been discussing a 30-day extension to the cease-fire and a reopening of the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.headline: Iran sends response to US ceasefire proposal via Pakistan
stories.summary: Tehran has been saying negotiations should focus on ending hostilities, securing guarantees against future attacks.

stories.headline: Iran sends response to US proposal to end war via mediator Pakistan
stories.summary: According to the proposal, initial negotiations will focus on ending hostilities.

stories.headline: Putin hints at ending Russia’s war in Ukraine, but why now?
stories.summary: Amid a US-backed ceasefire, Putin signals peace talks could progress, but broader negotiations remain stalled.

stories.headline: Stories of those killed in a single day of Israeli strikes on Lebanon
stories.summary: Israeli strikes have killed at least 39 people in a single day of attacks across Lebanon during a so-called ‘ceasefire.’

stories.headline: Putin claims war with Ukraine could be coming to an end
stories.summary: Putin declared the war in Ukraine was "heading to an end" on Saturday, even though the first day of a US-brokered ceasefire was marred by mutual accusations of violations and a scaled-down Victory Day parade.

stories.headline: At least 39 killed in fresh Israeli strikes on Lebanon
stories.summary: Despite a three-week-old ceasefire, Israel struck Lebanon repeatedly on Saturday killing at least thirty-nine people, while Hezbollah retaliated with drone attacks on northern Israel.

stories.headline: First plane carrying passengers evacuated from hantavirus-hit cruise ship leaves Tenerife – Europe live
stories.summary: Spain has begun the evacuation of the first passengers from the MV Hondius ship which arrived in Tenerife on SundayPassengers begin evacuating cruise ship hit by hantavirusIn other news, there are reports of Russian attacks continuing on Ukraine despite a ceasefire that was meant to run from Saturday 9 May to Monday 11 May.There are reports of people being injured by Russian strikes in areas including the Kharkiv, Dnipropetrovsk and Kherson regio

stories.headline: Russia kills three Ukrainians in 24 hours, accuses Kyiv of violating truce
stories.summary: Russia claims Ukraine has committed more than 1,000 violations since the three-day truce came into effect on Saturday.

stories.headline: Football on ruins: Gaza’s orphans find refuge on the pitch
stories.summary: A Gaza teenager orphaned by an Israeli attack finds refuge in football amid the decimation of Palestinian sports.

stories.headline: Iran war could prompt Federal Reserve to raise rates, Pimco says
stories.summary: Bond giant and Franklin Templeton warn in interviews with FT against cuts in borrowing costs

stories.headline: Trump’s war has given China an economic opening
stories.summary: Beijing could turn instability in the Middle East into a strategic gain

stories.headline: Trump, Xi Set to Meet for High-Stakes Meeting in Beijing
stories.summary: President Donald Trump said he’s looking forward to meeting Chinese counterpart Xi Jinping, signaling his plans for the high-stakes summit are still on despite fresh tensions between the world’s largest economies. Former Assistant Secretary of State for East Asia Danny Russel joins David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Pimco CIO Sees Risk of Fed Hiking Rates Due to Iran War, FT Says
stories.summary: The war in Iran may lead the Federal Reserve to further delay interest-rate cuts and instead raise rates, Pimco Chief Investment Officer Dan Ivascyn told the Financial Times.

stories.headline: Aramco CEO Warns of Long Oil Market Disruption as Profit Jumps
stories.summary: Saudi Aramco’s boss warned of a long disruption to oil markets from the near closure of the Strait of Hormuz, while the company reported a jump in profit following higher prices and its ability to redirect exports via a pipeline bypassing the vital waterway.

stories.headline: Republicans who denied 2020 election results could be governors next year
stories.summary: Winners will have oversight of 2028 elections in swing states like Arizona, Wisconsin and Pennsylvania.

stories.headline: ‘Amazon of America’: film paints vision of a post-coup Brazil giving up rainforest
stories.summary: Vitória Régia imagines rightwing Bolsonaro plot succeeded with US help – and  highlights threats facing Indigenous peoplesThe year is 2025 and far-right coup plotters have annihilated Brazil’s democracy, assassinating the president, closing the national congress and surrendering the Amazon rainforest and its untold riches to the United States.“Ladies and gentlemen, welcome to the Amazon of America,” a thick-accented North American soldier tells a

stories.headline: Tehran, Taiwan, trade … what are the hazards facing Trump on Xi summit tightrope?
stories.summary: US leader enters talks with superpower rival from vulnerable position, but will be hoping for economic wins amid turbulent backdropIf all goes to plan over the next few days – and that is a big if – Donald Trump will arrive in Beijing on Wednesday for a highly anticipated summit with Xi Jinping, China’s leader.The trip will mark the first time a US president has visited China in nearly a decade. The last visit was also made by Trump, during his f

stories.summary: This blog is now closedGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastWilson promises Coalition will be ‘very clear’ on migrationWilson is asked about comments made by the opposition leader, Angus Taylor, after the byelection that hinted at a rightward shift toward “ending mass migration” and stopping net zero policies.I can assure you in the coming weeks we’re going to make it very clear what we’re for. Australians need to know that

stories.headline: Trump visits China, vote on Sara Duterte impeachment, SoftBank earnings
Australia's right-wing One Nation party scores historic parliamentary win
stories.summary: One Nation gains its first seat in the lower house, in a by-election seen as a key test for the populist party.

stories.headline: Germany in fresh push to buy Tomahawks after Trump row
stories.summary: Defence minister plans Washington trip after Chancellor Friedrich Merz falls out with US president

stories.headline: Trump's Latest 10% Tariffs Declared Unlawful by US Trade
stories.summary: President Donald Trump’s 10% global tariffs were declared unlawful by a federal trade court in a fresh blow to the administration’s economic agenda, just months after the US Supreme Court vacated earlier levies he’d imposed. Crooked Media’s Straight Scrutiny Co-host and author of The U.S. Constitution: A Comprehensive and Annotated Guide for the Modern Reader Melissa Murray joins David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discu

stories.headline: Why Parenting Now Feels Like an Investment Strategy
stories.summary: In an age of hustle culture and hyperoptimization, parenting has become a source of pressure. Author of “Overinvested: The Emotional Economy of Modern Parenting” Nina Bandelj joins David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss the shift in modern-day parenting practices. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Spice Company Wins Suit Over 10% Trump Global Tariffs
stories.summary: President Donald Trump’s 10% global tariffs were declared unlawful by a federal trade court in a fresh blow to the administration’s economic agenda, just months after the US Supreme Court vacated earlier levies he’d imposed. Burlap and Barrel is a small spice importer and it's one of the plaintiffs that sued to get this result. Co-founders and Co-CEOs, Ethan Frisch and Ori Zohar and Liberty Justice Center Chairman & CEO Sara Albrecht join David G

stories.headline: Trump Administration Is Considering Pausing the Federal Gas Tax to Lower Prices, Energy Secretary Says
stories.summary: The tax is a little over 18 cents a gallon for gasoline. With the average national price above $4.50, the measure will provide little relief.

stories.headline: After Virginia Redistricting Map Is Tossed, Democrats Search Desperately for a Response
stories.summary: A conversation involving House members from Virginia and the top House Democrat reflected the fury and desperation that has gripped the party after Friday’s ruling in the state.

stories.headline: Democratic Candidates Scramble in Virginia After Court Tosses Map
stories.summary: A court ruling that struck down an election map swiftly ended some Democratic House candidacies and pushed others into much tougher races.

stories.headline: Vance or Rubio? Trump Muses on Successor as the ‘Kids’ Fill Bigger Roles.
stories.summary: Vice President JD Vance and Secretary of State Marco Rubio are boosting their profiles, generating speculation about who’s lining up for the 2028 presidential nomination.

stories.headline: Nationals MP Colin Boyce says he’s considering move to One Nation after ‘wake-up call’ in Farrer byelection
stories.summary: Exclusive: Queensland MP says ‘I think everybody should be thinking about their political future’ after devastating result for CoalitionGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastNationals MP Colin Boyce is considering shifting to One Nation after the Farrer byelection saw the Coalition’s vote tank to about 20% of the primary vote.Speaking to the Guardian in Albury after One Nation recorded its historic victory in the House of Rep

stories.headline: Atef Najib faces at least 10 charges in landmark Syria trial
stories.summary: Cousin of former President Bashar al-Assad faces charges that include murder, torture and responsibility for massacres.

stories.headline: China's EcoFlow to sell solar panels in British supermarkets soon
Led by industry, Japan and Taiwan plant seeds of drone cooperation
NATO and Japan weigh shared use of satellite launch sites
Remaking Hanoi: Vietnam bets on colossal capital renewal to drive growth
Brzoska: Poland establishes itself as a deregulation model for the EU
stories.summary: Poland could be a role model for Brussels on simplifying legislation and deregulation, Rafał Brzoska, one of Poland’s richest men, told Euronews at the European Economic Congress.

stories.headline: Spain's political clash that almost blocked hantavirus ship from Canary Islands
stories.summary: MV Hondius anchored off Granadilla early Sunday after a night of tension between the Canary Islands government and Madrid over evacuation flights, leading President Fernando Clavijo to threaten to block the stop.

stories.headline: Hungary’s next health minister Zsolt Hegedűs dances at Magyar celebration
stories.summary: Large crowds gathered outside the Hungarian parliament building to celebrate the official inauguration of Peter Magyar as prime minister. Attendees included incoming Health Minister Zsolt Hegedus, who went viral for his dancing on the night of the country's April election.

stories.headline: Spain’s climate shelters could save thousands of lives. Why is the rest of Europe lagging behind?
stories.summary: Climate shelters are becoming "critical components” of urban strategies as heat deaths in Europe continue to rise.

stories.headline: Lebanon says Israeli strikes killed 39
stories.summary: Israel and Hezbollah have continued to trade fire despite a ceasefire deal being announced last month.

stories.headline: International cyber attack disrupts swathe of universities and schools
stories.summary: A hacking group breached the academic software Canvas, used by thousands of schools and universities across the globe.

stories.headline: Traders Looking for Next Leg in Global Stocks Rally Bet on Asia
stories.summary: As the focus moves away from the Iran war, investors and strategists alike are looking for the next leg up in equities. Many are turning to Asia.

stories.headline: Mexican parents criticise ending school year a month early for World Cup
stories.summary: The federal government faces widespread anger for saying the school year could end on 5 June to accommodate the football tournament.

stories.headline: Japanese council votes to remove unconscious mayor
stories.summary: Kikuo Hatakeyama, 72, has served as the mayor of Hachirogata in north-east Japan since 2008, but fell ill in February.

stories.headline: Stock Trader’s Guide to Navigating High Stakes Trump-Xi Talks
stories.summary: Investors are looking for further signs of easing tensions between President Donald Trump and his counterpart Xi Jinping to help remove an overhang on Chinese markets, with geopolitical and trade issues in focus.

stories.headline: Australia to Tackle ‘Unacceptable’ Housing Market, Chalmers Says
stories.summary: Australia’s upcoming budget will take aim at soaring home prices and seek to make it easier for people to gain a foothold in the housing market, Treasurer Jim Chalmers said.

stories.headline: Bloomberg This Weekend 5/9/2026
stories.summary: The news doesn’t stop when markets close. Hosts David Gura, Christina Ruffini and Lisa Mateo bring clarity, context and a bit of humor to the weekend’s biggest headlines, LIVE from New York. Joined by Daniel Dae Kim, Host and Executive Producer of 'K-Everything,' Nicholas Burns, Former Ambassador to China and Professor of Diplomacy and International Relations at Harvard University, Gary Gensler, Former Chair, Commodity Futures Trading Commission

stories.headline: Kazakhstan strikes gold on day 2 in Astana
stories.summary: Day 2 of the Qazaqstan Barysy Grand Slam 2026 brought gold for the home crowd as Zhalgas Kairolla thrilled Astana. Enkhriilen Lkhagvatogoo, Danil Lavrentev and Clémence Eme also topped the podium, while Kazakhstan celebrated gold and bronze.

stories.headline: Cattle theft in Germany: organised gangs target farms
stories.summary: Livestock theft is becoming a problem in German agriculture, with entire herds disappearing overnight in some cases. In addition to financial losses, the offences also have an emotional impact on farmers. Investigators believe that organised gangs from abroad are involved.

stories.headline: The WW2 spy killed in mystery crash days after the war ended
stories.summary: Jacques Vaillant de Guélis was born in Cardiff but served as a French operative behind enemy lines.

stories.headline: Three dead after volcano erupts on Indonesian island
stories.summary: Officials had previously issued warnings on climbing the active volcano due to safety concerns.

stories.headline: Four south Florida men convicted in Haitian president’s assassination
stories.summary: Men were convicted in Miami federal court for plotting to kill Jovenel Moïse at his Port-au-Prince home in 2021Four south Florida men were convicted on Friday of plotting to kill the Haitian president, Jovenel Moïse, in 2021 by hiring mercenaries to assassinate him at his Port-au-Prince home, court records show.Prosecutors argued during the nine-week trial in a Miami federal court that the men assembled two dozen former Colombian soldiers and sup

stories.headline: From trusted aide to biggest rival: Suvendu Adhikari becomes West Bengal chief minister
stories.summary: A former ally of Mamata Banerjee, the BJP leader will be West Bengal’s next chief minister.

stories.headline: Judge rules DOGE’s cuts to humanities grants were unconstitutional
stories.summary: A federal judge said more than $100 million in cuts to NEH grants were discriminatory, ruling in a case that revealed the inner workings of DOGE.

stories.headline: Hong Kong dissident Nathan Law on China spies in UK: ‘We’re not surprised’
stories.summary: Law says conviction of two men for spying raises serious concerns about how they accessed sensitive informationNathan Law, an exiled leader of the Hong Kong student protest who lives with a £100,000 bounty on his head from the Chinese authorities, was not surprised to discover a spy ring had photographed him entering the Oxford Union for an evening debate in November 2023.The conviction at the Old Bailey of Chi Leung “Peter” Wai, 38, and Chung Bi

stories.headline: Iran considering US proposal as Trump says war will be 'over quickly'
stories.summary: Mediator Pakistan says it is "endeavouring to convert this ceasefire into a permanent end to this war".

stories.headline: Fears of renewed Gaza war as Hamas disarmament talks stall
stories.summary: Israeli media reports suggest Israel is preparing to resume fighting because of the impasse in talks with Hamas.

stories.headline: Ukraine war 'led me to surrogacy' to earn money but a new law could end those plans
stories.summary: Ukraine is considering a law that would effectively ban foreigners from accessing surrogacy in the country.

stories.headline: Trump's 'irresponsible war' to blame for economic slowdown, German minister says
stories.summary: Finance Minister Lars Klingbeil blamed the US president's "irresponsible war in Iran" for harming the Germany economy.

stories.headline: Anti-war protests rock Japan as PM pushes for stronger defence
stories.summary: Japan's largest anti-war protests in decades highlight a population divided over changes to its pacifist constitution.

stories.headline: Trump gives EU ultimatum deadline to approve trade deal with US
stories.summary: Trump's deadline to the European Union came as a trade court ruled his global tariff policy violated US law.

stories.headline: Disturbances and 127 arrests mar Paris party after PSG Champions League victory
stories.summary: Fans took to the streets to celebrate the Paris team's qualification for the final, but celebrations were marred by disturbances.

stories.headline: China announces suspended death sentences for former defence ministers
stories.summary: The announcement follows recent ousting of several top officials in China's military.

stories.headline: Crowds cheer China's new snooker star on return from championship win
stories.summary: Wu Yize, 22, receives a rock star's welcome after becoming second-youngest player to win a world title.

stories.headline: Top BJP leader's aide shot dead in violence after Indian state election
stories.summary: Chandranath Rath was personal assistant to Suvendu Adhikari, front runner to become West Bengal's new chief minister.

stories.headline: Woman jailed in Somalia for peaceful protest ‘stripped, kicked and beaten’
stories.summary: In an exclusive interview from prison, Sadia Moalim Ali, a 27-year-old rickshaw driver, tells of her treatment after being arrested for demonstrating against the governmentA woman being held in prison in Somalia for taking part in peaceful protests has described how she was tortured by her guards.Sadia Moalim Ali, 27, told the Guardian she was stripped naked by two male guards in a room monitored by CCTV, kicked, beaten with a baton and left for

stories.headline: 'Enjoy the show. Ignore the war': Venice Biennale faces backlash after including Russia
stories.summary: Protests have taken place at the prestigious arts fair over the inclusion of Russia for the first time since the full-scale invasion of Ukraine.

stories.headline: Trump says US to pause operation to guide vessels through Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: "Project Freedom", which began on Monday, will be halted because progress has been made toward a deal with Iran, the US president says.

stories.headline: What is Trump's 'Project Freedom' in the Strait of Hormuz?
stories.summary: The US president said the operation has been paused after starting on Monday.

stories.headline: Only one of Berkshire Hathaway and SoftBank can survive
stories.summary: The two represent competing visions of the future

stories.headline: Bowen: Strait of Hormuz standoff raises risk of sliding back into all-out war
stories.summary: The US and Iran's determination to keep the pressure on each other has put the fragile Gulf ceasefire in serious jeopardy.

stories.headline: Nine coal miners die in gas explosion in Colombia
stories.summary: The explosion is the latest accident in the locality of Sutatausa, the site of another deadly mine disaster in 2023.

stories.headline: Global carmakers desperately want to be more Chinese
stories.summary: But partnering with local companies carries big risks

stories.headline: The remarkable revival of eBay
stories.summary: The internet’s flea market is back—and GameStop wants to buy it
